examples.title: Inorder Traversal in Threaded Binary Tree
examples.description: Algorithm to traverse a doubly-threaded binary tree in inorder without using recursion or stack
examples.explanation: Starting from leftmost node, we visit each node and check if a thread to successor exists (rthread=1). If yes, we directly jump to successor. If no, we go to right child and then find its leftmost descendant. This visits all nodes in inorder sequence without recursion or stack.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - each node visited exactly once
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only uses pointer variables, no stack or auxiliary data structure

examples.title: Insertion in Threaded Binary Search Tree
examples.description: Insert a new node in a threaded binary search tree maintaining thread connections
examples.explanation: We traverse like a BST to find the insertion point. When we find a thread (NULL child), we insert the new node there. The new node's threads are set to connect to its inorder predecessor and successor. The parent's flags are updated to indicate it now has a child.
examples.timeComplexity: O(h) where h is height of tree. Worst case O(n) for skewed tree.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only uses a few pointer variables besides the new node

examples.title: Finding Inorder Successor in Threaded Tree
examples.description: Algorithm to find inorder successor of a given node in a doubly-threaded binary tree
examples.code:
FUNCTION FindSuccessor(node)
    IF node = NULL THEN
        RETURN NULL
    END IF
    
    // If right thread exists, successor is directly reachable
    IF node.rthread = 1 THEN
        RETURN node.right
    END IF
    
    // Otherwise, go to right child, then find leftmost
    successor = node.right
    WHILE successor.lthread = 0 DO
        successor = successor.left
    END WHILE
    
    RETURN successor
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: The algorithm checks if a thread to successor exists (rthread=1). If yes, return that pointer directly in O(1). If no, we must go to the right child and descend left until we find the leftmost node, which is the inorder successor. This mirrors how inorder traversal works.
examples.timeComplexity: O(h) in worst case where we need to descend, O(1) if thread exists
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only uses constant extra space
